Est. 1890

A Legacy of Healing

Honoring the visionary founder, Dr. Jammi Venkataramanayya. Over a century of bridging ancient Ayurvedic wisdom with the rigor of modern science to serve humanity.

Dr. Jammi Venkataramanayya

The Visionary Founder

The Architect of Modern Ayurveda

A Life Dedicated to Healing

In the late 19th century, when modern medicine was still in its infancy in India, Dr. Jammi Venkataramanayya embarked on a mission to democratize the profound healing wisdom of Ayurveda.

Recognizing the need for standardization, he became one of the first practitioners to introduce scientific rigor into the preparation of Ayurvedic formulations, ensuring consistent quality and efficacy for every patient.

"True healing occurs only when the ancient texts are honored with modern precision."

A Century of Milestones

1890

Founding in Vijayanagaram

Dr. Jammi Venkataramanayya begins his quest to modernize traditional healing practices with his first clinic.

1940

Tamil Nadu Govt Award

Recognition for pioneering work in public health and standardized Ayurveda during a critical healthcare crisis.

1965

The First Manufacturing Unit

Establishment of a dedicated facility ensuring all formulations meet strict clinical standards.

1990

Premier Institute Collaborations

Jammi partners with technical institutes (IITs) to validate ancient molecules using modern analytics.

2025

A New Digital Frontier

Bringing 128 years of clinical heritage to patients globally through advanced online consultation platforms.

The Archival Gallery

Glimpses into the foundational days of Jammi Pharmaceuticals.

Circa 1905

Sacred Formulations

Original handwritten notes documenting precise ingredient ratios for potent remedies.

Circa 1920

Apothecary Dispensary

First-generation glass tincture bottles used for storing delicate active botanical extracts.

Circa 1965

The First Laboratory

Our shift into standardized mass production while maintaining strict clinical protocols.

"Medicine is not an experiment. It is a legacy. 128 years of proof that authentic healthcare belongs to the practitioners of India."
